title: 267. Låt oljan flöda
summary_md:
# 267. Låt oljan flöda

**Feed**: aktiepodden  
**Published**: 2026-06-19  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

Episode 267 of Aktiepodden is dominated by macro: a fragile US-Iran ceasefire ('Västgöta-klimax'), the Riksbank holding rates at 1.75% while nudging up the rate path, and BMW's profit warning blamed on China and the oil crisis. The hosts then cover several CEO departures (Ericsson's Börje Ekholm, Bure's CEO, Hexatronic's datacenter head) and review the Securitas and Elekta capital markets days, favouring Securitas as the more solid case. An appended CEO interview with Prostatype Genomics rounds out the episode.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 SECU-B — Securitas | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Strong capital markets day: 10% EPS-growth target, tech/software shift, datacenter security +30%/yr, raised cash conversion target; host's preferred pick of the two.

#### Reasoning

Hosts view Securitas as a stable business leveraging its global market position into higher-value tech and software, raising revenue per customer. Datacenter security (~6% of sales, growing ~30%/yr) benefits from the AI buildout. New financial targets (10% EPS growth, organic 4-6%, continued margin improvement, deleveraging plus bolt-ons) seen as solid; host says he would buy Securitas over Elekta.

#### Sell Triggers

- if organic growth in technical solutions falls below the new ~6% guidance
- if margin improvement stalls below ~0.2-0.3pp/yr
- if datacenter security growth decelerates

> *"Skulle jag köpa en aktie av de här två på det vi fått höra under onsdagens kapitalmarknadsdagar så är det väl ändå Securitas som kändes mer solid av dem."*

---

### 🟠 EKTA-B — Elekta | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 40%

Capital markets day: long-term margin target raised to 14-16% but near-term FY26/27 cut to 12.5-13.5%; market took the negative read. Binary on US organic growth turnaround.

#### Reasoning

Glass half-full vs half-empty: bulls see a raised long-term margin target, skeptics see weaker near-term margins sweetened by distant promises. The stock is heavily punished and the case hinges on reversing US organic growth, making it binary. Last report was strong, but without proof of a US turnaround the hosts are cautious; one likes it, the other is skeptical.

#### Sell Triggers

- if US organic growth fails to inflect
- if near-term margins disappoint vs 12.5-13.5% guidance
- if the trust gap around the company persists

> *"Marknaden tog fasta på den lite negativa halvfulla glas-teorin."*

---

### 🟠 HTRO — Hexatronic | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 40%

Vice CEO who heads the datacenter segment — the unit driving the stock's rally — is stepping down, while legacy fiber-to-home (Fiber Solutions) has stalled.

#### Reasoning

Hosts are concerned because the executive leaving runs datacenter, the segment whose extremely strong growth has powered the share's recent rally. The older fiber-to-home business has decelerated and seems hard to revive given high Nordic penetration. Loss of the segment head is a setback and the succession is the key uncertainty to watch.

#### Sell Triggers

- if datacenter growth slows after the leadership change
- if Fiber Solutions continues to decline without recovery
- if succession destabilises the segment

> *"det stora här som gör en lite orolig på den här pressmeddelandet är ju att han är chef för verksamhetet datacenter. Och det är ju det här segmentet som lyfter hela Hexatronic i nuläget."*

---

### 🟠 PROG — Prostatype Genomics | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 40%

CEO interview (guest opinion): SEK 47m rights issue ~70% secured for US expansion, pending Medicare reimbursement approval at ~$3,800/test; sees sale or partnership within ~2 years.

#### Reasoning

CEO Fredrik Rickman is strongly bullish: the prostate cancer biomarker test is validated and launched in Europe and the US, with all commercial/regulatory/lab/billing investments already made, awaiting Medicare reimbursement within the existing LCD framework at an attractive ~$3,800/test. He expects approval soon to materially lift the share and anticipates a partnership or sale within two years. This is promotional guest conviction, not host analysis — speculative pending the regulatory catalys

#### Sell Triggers

- if Medicare reimbursement approval is denied or further delayed
- if the rights issue is not fully subscribed
- if no US partner or acquirer materialises

> *"Det är självklart så att ett Medicare-godkännande kommer att påverka priset på våra aktier. På ett positivt sätt."*

---
